metadata.note: All 3 evals: LIVE delegation subagent runs. No analytical stubs. Self-benchmark -- skill graded on its own methodology.

expectations.text: Response provides the complete YAML metadata block with all required fields: author, version, category, origin, homepage, author-github
expectations.evidence: Full YAML block present with all six required fields correctly formatted.

expectations.text: Response provides the exact header line format: '**OverKill Hill P³** · [overkillhill.com](...) · [github.com/OKHP3](...)'
expectations.evidence: Exact match: '**OverKill Hill P³** · [overkillhill.com](https://overkillhill.com) · [github.com/OKHP3](https://github.com/OKHP3)'

expectations.text: Response provides the exact About footer with all three lines: 'Built by...', 'Published at...', 'Part of the OKHP3/skillz...'
expectations.evidence: All three About footer lines present and exactly correct.

expectations.text: Response specifies the version bump rule: bumping from 1.0.0 to 1.1.0 (minor) for brand attribution as a structural change
expectations.evidence: 'Note: The version is set to 1.1.0 because adding brand attribution to an existing skill is considered a structural change.'

expectations.text: Response provides the complete YAML metadata block with all required fields: author, version, category, origin, homepage, author-github
expectations.evidence: YAML uses non-standard fields (brand, author: OKHP3 Skill Foundry). Missing category, origin, homepage, author-github.

expectations.text: Response provides the exact header line format: '**OverKill Hill P³** · [overkillhill.com](...) · [github.com/OKHP3](...)'
expectations.evidence: Said to add '# OKHP3 Skill: weather-fetch' as header. Correct inline branded format completely absent.

expectations.text: Response provides the exact About footer with all three lines: 'Built by...', 'Published at...', 'Part of the OKHP3/skillz...'
expectations.evidence: About section titled '## About this Skill', contains fabricated 'OKHP3 (Open Knowledge Host Protocol v3)' and dead URL 'https://okhp.org/docs'. None of the three required lines present.

expectations.text: Response specifies the version bump rule: bumping from 1.0.0 to 1.1.0 (minor) for brand attribution as a structural change
expectations.evidence: Version bump rule not mentioned. YAML shows version: 1.0.0 with no explanation.

expectations.text: Response designs exactly 3 test cases, each with a distinct concrete capability (not variations of the same thing)
expectations.evidence: 3 cases: basic-fetch-implementation (URL construction), function-signature-and-return-shape (TypeScript return type), integration-and-unit-handling (celsius constraint). Distinct.

expectations.text: Response writes exactly 4 expectations per test case
expectations.evidence: Eval 1: 4 expectations. Eval 2: 4 expectations. Eval 3: 4 expectations. All labeled 'Evidence-Anchored'.

expectations.text: At least one expectation per test case anchors to a specific identifier from the skill: fetchWeather, temperature_2m, wind_speed_10m, or the exact return shape { temperature, windSpeed, unit: 'celsius' }
expectations.evidence: Eval 1: 'current=temperature_2m,wind_speed_10m' and 'fetchWeather'. Eval 2: 'unit: 'celsius''. Eval 3: '{ temperature: number, windSpeed: number, unit: 'celsius' }'.

expectations.text: Response explains the evidence-anchoring requirement: that without-skill should fail most expectations, and that expectations must reference skill-specific content not derivable from general LLM knowledge
expectations.evidence: Opening states: 'exactly 4 evidence-anchored expectations designed to discriminate between general LLM knowledge and skill-specific implementation.'

expectations.text: Response designs exactly 3 test cases, each with a distinct concrete capability (not variations of the same thing)
expectations.evidence: 3 distinct cases: Direct Location Query, Coordinate-Based Request, Handling Invalid Input.

expectations.text: Response writes exactly 4 expectations per test case
expectations.evidence: 4 expectations per case. Count correct.

expectations.text: At least one expectation per test case anchors to a specific identifier from the skill: fetchWeather, temperature_2m, wind_speed_10m, or the exact return shape { temperature, windSpeed, unit: 'celsius' }
expectations.evidence: None of 12 expectations reference fetchWeather, temperature_2m, wind_speed_10m, or unit: 'celsius'. All test general agent behaviors (Tool Call Verification, Natural Language Response, Graceful Degradation) that would pass for any weather API.

expectations.text: Response explains the evidence-anchoring requirement: that without-skill should fail most expectations, and that expectations must reference skill-specific content not derivable from general LLM knowledge
expectations.evidence: No mention of evidence anchoring, without-skill baseline, or discrimination requirement. The word 'evidence' does not appear in the response.

expectations.text: Response grades the expectation as FAILED (not passed) -- the executor used 'lat'/'lon' not 'latitude'/'longitude', used hourly not current, and used temperature_2m in hourly context not current context
expectations.evidence: 'Decision: FAIL' with numbered reasoning: '1. It used lat and lon instead of the required latitude and longitude parameter names. 2. It used the hourly=temperature_2m parameter instead of the required current=temperature_2m,wind_speed_10m parameter.'

expectations.text: Response writes a grading.json entry with the exact fields: text, passed, evidence
expectations.evidence: grading.json shows fields: 'text', 'passed': false, 'evidence'. All three Foundry-specified field names present and correctly used.

expectations.text: Response quotes verbatim from the mock executor response as the evidence string (not a paraphrase)
expectations.evidence: Evidence field contains: 'Response provided "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?lat=\"\+lat+...&hourly=temperature_2m" which uses incorrect parameter names...' -- actual mock text quoted.

expectations.text: Response identifies at least two specific deviations: the parameter name difference (lat vs latitude) AND the hourly vs current context difference
expectations.evidence: Both deviations explicitly numbered and named.

expectations.text: Response grades the expectation as FAILED (not passed) -- the executor used 'lat'/'lon' not 'latitude'/'longitude', used hourly not current, and used temperature_2m in hourly context not current context
expectations.evidence: 'Result: Fail' with correct reasoning.

expectations.text: Response writes a grading.json entry with the exact fields: text, passed, evidence
expectations.evidence: grading.json uses 'expectation' instead of 'text', and 'explanation' instead of 'evidence'. Actual: {"expectation": "...", "passed": false, "explanation": "..."}. Foundry field names 'text' and 'evidence' are not present.

expectations.text: Response quotes verbatim from the mock executor response as the evidence string (not a paraphrase)
expectations.evidence: 'Actual: https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?lat="+lat+"&lon="+lon+"&hourly=temperature_2m' -- quoted from mock.

expectations.text: Response identifies at least two specific deviations: the parameter name difference (lat vs latitude) AND the hourly vs current context difference
expectations.evidence: Identifies both: 'lat and lon' vs 'latitude and longitude', and 'hourly parameter' vs 'current parameter with temperature_2m and wind_speed_10m'.

notes: All 3 evals: LIVE delegation subagent runs. No analytical stubs.
Self-benchmark -- the Foundry skill graded on its own methodology. Meta.
Eval 1: without-skill fabricated 'OKHP3 (Open Knowledge Host Protocol v3)' -- wrong expansion. Dead URL. 0/4 -- total failure on brand standard.
Eval 2: without-skill eval design produces non-discriminating expectations (test general agent behavior, not skill-specific content). A developer following this pattern would see no with/without gap.
Eval 3: without-skill gets the pass/fail right (LLMs understand endpoint mismatch) but uses wrong grading.json field names -- schema incompatibility.
Acceptance thresholds: with_skill mean 1.0 >= 0.9 PASS. delta +0.58 >= 0.5 PASS. Skill is production-ready.
